title: Node 24, TypeScript 7.0.2, TypeScript 6 API Compatibility, npm, and Reproducible Installs · CI/CD Retrieval
summary: An operations handoff reconstructs node 24, typescript 7.0.2, typescript 6 api compatibility, npm, and reproducible installs from delayed run evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the reproducible TypeScript build contract under changed load. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reproducible TypeScript build contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Align Node 24, npm 11 or later, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API required by current tooling, package metadata, engines, action runtime, and local developer versions.
Use npm ci with a committed matching lockfile and committed install flags while detecting lock drift and registry changes.
Bound dependency lifecycle scripts, registry authentication, private package access, network egress, install auditing, and secret exposure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract delivery-system evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract, keep Ci Node Npm Typescript Version Contract connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A review TypeScript build floats Node and npm, mutates its lockfile, runs dependency lifecycle scripts, calls a programmatic API that TypeScript 7 does not provide, and passes only with warm local residue. Prove Node 24, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, npm ci, clean output, and changed-environment behavior. Competency probe: Align Node 24, npm 11 or later, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API required by current tooling, package metadata, engines, action runtime, and local developer versions.
